behaviors.description: Only files changed in the PR are linted; unchanged files are not analyzed
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: mocking `get_changed_files()` would hide bugs in GitHub API integration, file response parsing, or how we construct the query. REAL-INFRA: must verify the code correctly calls GitHub's changed-files API (or git diff equivalent) and filters to exactly those files. Unit tests of the filtering logic alone wouldn't catch API integration failures.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the GitHub API response to return exactly 3 changed files {A, B, C}, run the linter, verify via call inspection or log output that only those 3 files are analyzed and no others from the repo are touched.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is necessary because the failure mode spans both GitHub API integration and filtering logic, not just the filter itself in isolation.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the PR comment body
behaviors.justification: Pure data transformation with no infrastructure dependency. Failure 'findings not grouped correctly' can be caught entirely by testing the grouping/formatting logic with mocked linter output. No real files, API, or subprocess needed.
behaviors.test_description: Mock linter findings with mixed severities (e.g., 2 errors, 3 warnings, 1 info), invoke the grouping/comment-formatting function, verify the returned PR comment body has errors first, then warnings, then info, each section clearly labeled.

behaviors.description: Analyzing 200 changed files completes within the 10-minute GitHub Actions timeout
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA and MOCK-MISS: mocking file I/O or linter execution entirely hides wall-clock performance bottlenecks. Real failure is timeout under real load. Must actually run the linter on real files and measure elapsed time; local integration testing might miss GitHub Actions environment differences (runner slowness, resource contention).
behaviors.test_description: Create or use 200 realistic test files with varied sizes and complexity, run the full pipeline (fetch changed files, analyze each, post PR comment) with the actual linter executable and real GitHub API call (or mock server with realistic latency), measure total elapsed time, assert completion in < 600 seconds.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System level is necessary; Integration alone (local linter + mocked API) might miss timing issues specific to the GitHub Actions environment.

behaviors.description: When linter crashes on a malformed file, remaining files are still analyzed and results are posted
behaviors.justification: MOCK-HIDE: mocking linter exceptions would hide whether the real linter actually crashes the way expected or leaves process state corrupted. Real failure is 'pipeline stops when linter encounters malformed file.' Need to run the real linter on real malformed input to verify error handling works end-to-end.
behaviors.test_description: Create a malformed file (e.g., syntactically invalid code) that causes the real linter to crash, include it in a batch with valid files, run the pipeline, verify: (1) crash is caught and logged, (2) remaining valid files are still analyzed, (3) partial results are posted to the PR comment without stopping.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is preferred over Unit because real linter crash behavior may differ from mocked exceptions; ensures error handling works with actual failures.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output
behaviors.justification: Pure deduplication logic with no infrastructure dependency. Failure 'duplicates not removed' can be caught entirely with mocked linter findings. The real linter's output format doesn't affect the test; we only need to verify the dedup function works on any input structure.
behaviors.test_description: Mock linter findings where rule A and rule B both report a finding on line 42 of the same file, invoke the deduplication function, verify exactly one finding is returned for line 42 (not two), and check correctness of the retained finding (message, severity, etc.).
